name: Ms. Swayamprava Mishra
qualification: Basis Bsc.Nursing, Masters in Obstetrics and Gynaecological Nursing
research_interests: Related to Medical/Nursing Researches
bio: Swayamprava Mishra, is a dedicated nursing professional with a strong academic background and with five years of diverse experience in the nursing education and training field. She holds a B.Sc. Nursing degree from SUM Nursing College, SOA University, one of Odisha’s premier institution and M.Sc. Nursing degree from Sarvodaya College of Nursing, Bangalore.

name: Dr. Neeraj Kumar Sethiya
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
qualification: Ph. D in Pharmacy (March: 2012) from The MS University of Baroda, Vadodara, Gujarat, India.
specialisation: Pharmacognosy, Phytopharmacology and Herbal Drug Technology
research_interests: Lead based design and development of New Chemical Entities (NCEs) from natural scaffolds against Anti-stress, Anxiolytic, Anti-arthritis, Antidiabetic, Antioxidant, Alzheimer’s, Antimalarial, Hepatoprotective and Antiurolithiatic. He is currently working on the area of herbal nutraceuticals, herbal cosmetics and value added product development from orga
bio: Dr. Sethiya is one of leading academician and highly cited researcher (Name enlisted in Stanford top 2% database from last three years) in field of pharmacy specially pharmacognosy with more than 16 years of rich diversified experience including academia, industries and entrepreneurship in reputed government and private organization. Dr. Sethiya is an accomplished administrator leading to establishment of CIIIES (Centre of Innovation, Incubation, Entrepreneurship and Startup) and Institutional Innovation Council (IIC) at DIT University. He is recipient of many acclaimed awards including appreciation letter from Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi ji for parikisha pe charcha 2023 program, APTI-Uttrakhand Researcher, Excellence in Performance, SPER Young Scientist, Dr. PD Sethi Best Research Paper and UGC-JRF Research Award. He is also acting as consultant for various industrial project and principal investigator for one Govt and some private funded project. He framed curriculum and introduced Clinical Pharmacognosy, first time as value added subject for B. Pharm students at DIT University. Besides teaching pharmacognosy and natural product chemistry, he has also hands-on domain and expertise on innovation, incubation, entrepreneurship development, patents, and national biodiversity authority approval. He is an expert speaker for many conferences, workshop, FDP and webinar. Additionally, he is recognized Mentor for Atal Innovation Mission, IIT Mumbai, IIT Kharagpur, IIM Bodhgaya, Member Advisory Board Yuva Incubates, Startup India, Startup Uttarakhand, OP Jindal University, B. Incubate and Innovation Ambassador for Innovation Council, Ministry of Education, Govt of India. He has been recently Honoured with position of Editor in Chief for Bentham Sciences Journal Current Indian Sciences- Natural Product and The Journal of Phytopharmacology. Recently Dr. Sethiya has been appointed as Reviewer Credit Ambassador of India. He is also serving as a life member for various prestigious national and international associations.

name: Dhruva Chaudhary
qualification: B.E., M. Tech (Uttarakhand Technical University), Ph.D. (DIT   University)
specialisation: Analog and Digital Electronics, Signal Processing and Communication
research_interests: Communication, Antenna and IoT.
courses_taught: Digital Logic and computer design and Cyber security
Data Communication and Signals and systems
bio: Dr Dhruva Chaudhary is currently Assistant Professor-I in the School of Computing, DITU (erstwhile Dehradun Institute of Technology). He joined this prestigious institution in March 2010. Dr. Dhruva received his Ph.D. in the field of Antenna from DITU, and his M.Tech in Digital communication from Uttarakhand Technical University, Dehradun. He completed his B.E. in ECE from Kumaun University Nainital.

name: Rakesh K. Pandey
qualification: M. Tech - IIT (ISM), Dhanbad; Ph.D. - DIT University, Dehradun
research_interests: Applied Data Science in Oil and Gas Operations
bio: Dr. Pandey has worked on several industry projects in prominent petroleum companies such as ONGC, OIL, Vedanta, and Essar, which complements his academic expertise. His involvement in projects related to downhole well-test data acquisition and interpretation, echometer surveys, dynamometer surveys, and slickline operations has given him practical insights into real-world engineering challenges and enhanced his understanding of the industry's operational complexities. Furthermore, he has actively contributed to the field of education by playing a pivotal role in DIT University's Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC), where he has been instrumental in implementing outcome-based education practices. His commitment to quality is exemplified through his successful conduction of ISO internal audits, ensuring excellence within the institution. Dr. Pandey has been honored with accolades, including the Excellence in Research and Best Teacher Awards for his exceptional contributions in advancing knowledge, both through his research and his commitment to educating and mentoring students.

name: Prof. Ekta Singh
qualification: B.Arch (AKTU, Lko), M.S (BITS-Pilani), M.Des (JMI, New Delhi), Ph.D (IIT, Roorkee), FIIA
research_interests: Sustainable Design, Participatory Design, Social Design, Responsive Built Environment, System Thinking, Design Thinking Applications
bio: As an alumna of prestigious institutions like AKTU Lucknow, IIT Roorkee, BITS Pilani, and Jamia Milia New Delhi, Dr. Singh's rich cultural exposure enhances her role as a Multiverse designer, fostering contextual and empathetic design principles. Recognized as a judge for the CII-Design Awards at Goa, where she along with a panel of esteemed jury from across the globe evaluated the various industry & student’s design entries. She is a MASHAV Fellow and had received International Scholarship from the State of Israel, and holds distinguished fellowship of Indian Institute of Architects (IIA), and Member of Association of Designers in India (ADI). She has been on the jury boards of different National-Level Architecture, Design & Planning competitions. She had been associated as consultant with UP Government on prestigious ‘Namami Gange’ project of GOI. Having occupied diverse roles across various institutions, she demonstrates a profound grasp of the design education landscape.

name: Naveen Singhal
qualification: M.Sc. CCS University, Meerut, Ph.D - University of Roorkee
research_interests: Organic Electrochemistry ,Petroleum Processing ,Cementation ,Biochemistry , Nano materials and fibers , Environmental Monitoring
bio: I am working here since the inception of the Organization. I have worked on several portfolio in this university. I was head of the department in chemistry for 20 years. I was also the controller of examination for 15 years. Presently I am working as the Dean Alumni relations and working since last five years on this position. I am also coordinating NSS and NCC at state level. I have arranged so many activities in the University independently at state level and national level. Being chief proctor of the University I am fully engaged with students related to their hostel problems, discipline issues and other personal issues. I am also involved in the establishment of R & D activities and new courses in the chemistry department. I am running a center of excellence LAW in the University. At present I have published more than 30 research papers. Presently I am working with a project sponsored by UCOST with Dr. Jabrinder Singh.

name: Samir Bhargava
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
qualification: PhD (Pharmacy): Uttarakhand Technical University
research_interests: Drug Delivery, SSF and Liposome Development
bio: Samir Bhargava, is an Assistant Professor at Faculty of Pharmacy, DIT University. He is associated with University since 8 August 2006. As a researcher he has cross-disciplinary interests in drug delivery and fermentation technology. He completed his B Pharm in 2004 (SGRRITS, Dehradun); Qualified GATE in 2004. He has recently received a - Best Oral Presentation Award in 2019 from APTI at 24 Annual Convention. Recently, his one of Research Interest on peptide has been presented at National Biotechnology Conference (Hosted by American Association of Pharmaceutical Scientist, USA) in 2021. He has Co- guided more than 11 PG (M Pharm -Pharmaceutics) Student. With cross disciplinary focus, he is guiding 3 Research Scholars through his guidance.

name: Dr. Amit K. Dubey
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
qualification: Ph. D (worked in ICMR and awarded from NIPER)
specialisation: Pharmaceutical Biotechnology, Pharmaceutical Biochemistry, Pharmaceutical Microbiology, Novel drug delivery systems, Clinical Pharmacoinformatics, Pharmacovigilance, Target drug delivery systems
research_interests: Molecular medicine, Nano-therapeutics, Nano sensing, Gene therapy, Diagnostics kits, and Vaccines designs etc.
courses_taught: Pharmaceutical Microbiology Novel drug delivery system
bio: Amit K Dubey worked as a researcher in the Indian Council of Medical Research, Patna from 2020 to 2021. Earlier, he worked as Senior Research Fellow from 2017 to 2020 and Junior Research fellow from 2015-2017 from the National Institute of Pharmaceutical Education and Research. He obtained M.S. (Pharm.) (Biotech) from NIPER and Doctoral thesis work on Targeting and Validation of Novel Nucleoporin NUP93/NIC96 Superfamily Protein in Leishmania donovani and Its Inhibition via siRNA Knockdown, Molecular Dynamics, and Nanomedicine” His research interest is in Pharmaceutical Biotechnology, Molecular biology, Microbiology, Immunology, Clinical Research, and Nanotechnology. Also, siRNA gene silencing (Knockdown) and Drug/Vaccine designing are his expertise. He also attended various conferences and workshops in diverse fields such as Cancer, Diabetes, Parasitology, etc. He also worked on project ‘In silico epitope mapping of the signal peptide of Activated C protein: a candidate study of sepsis. Other dept. projects completed such as a nano-molecular vaccine for MDR strains or skin infections, A herbal gene therapy for brain meningitis, A pH responsive quantum medicine for anticancer therapeutics.

name: Anil Kumar
qualification: M.Tech [Delhi College of Engineering], PhD Sikkim Manipal Institute of Technology
specialisation: Computer Science and Engineering-Data Science, Deep Learning, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Cryptography, IOT, Image Processing
bio: Dr. Anil Kumar is currently working as a Professor CSE, Accreditation Coordinator, and Head-Data Science Research Group, DIT University. Prof. Anil Kumar received his M.Tech from Delhi College of Engineering, Delhi, and Ph.D. from Manipal Group. He has more than 24 years of teaching and industrial experience. He served various reputed origination like Manipal University, Bharti Vidyapeeth, Mody University Science and Technology, DRDO, etc. He is an IEEE Senior Member, ACM also worked as Executive Committee Member, IEEE Computer Society India Council 2015 & 2016, Currently, working as Executive Committee Member, IEEE Rajasthan Sub-Section. He has guided 10 Ph.D. Research Scholars. His research interests include Image processing algorithms, Cryptography, Artificial Intelligence, Signal and System, Neural Systems, and Genetic algorithms.

name: Amit Chatterjee
qualification: M.E. Electronics (Gold Medallist), PhD-IIT Indore (MEITY VISVESVARAYA Fellow), C.V. Raman Post-Doctoral Fellow IISC-Bangalore
specialisation: Image Processing, Biometrics, Optical Instruments, Artificial Intelligence
research_interests: Biometric Presentation Attack Detection, Smart Agriculture, Optical Signal Processing
bio: Amit Chatterjee joined the School of Computing as an Assistant Professor in February 2024. He holds a Bachelor's degree (B. Tech) from Asansol Engineering College (AEC), West Bengal, and a Master's degree (ME) from the Institute of Engineering and Technology (IET), Indore. Chatterjee earned his PhD in electrical engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Indore, garnering recognition with the MEITY Visvesvaraya Fellowship and the prestigious C V Raman Fellowship from IISC-Bangalore during his academic journey. With a focus on optical instrumentation, signal and image processing, biometric forgery detection, and smart agriculture, he is a dedicated member of the academic community. A life member of the Instrumentation Society of India (ISOI), he has made substantial contributions, including 12 peer-reviewed international journal papers, 25 conference presentations, and five patents. Notably, Dr. Chatterjee has a single-authored book, "Biometric Presentation Attack Detection: Towards Securing the Biometric Authentication Systems," with IOP Science (UK).

name: Aanchal Sharma
department: Architecture and Planning
qualification: B. Arch- Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee: Roorkee, Uttar Pradesh, Ph.D. IIT Roorkee
research_interests: Green Buildings, Climate Responsive Architectural Design, Energy Efficiency in Buildings, Sustainability in Built Environment, Indoor Thermal Comfort , Whole Building Energy Simulation, Post Occupancy Evaluation, Energy Efficient Retrofitting, Natural Ventilation, Adaptive Thermal Comfort, Impact of Land Use Land Coverage Change on Built Environment.
bio: Dr. Aanchal Sharma is an architect with experience in the various domains of the architecture profession including architectural practice, research, and academic teaching. Has obtained Ph.D. in Green Buildings from the Department of Architecture and Planning, IIT Roorkee, India which focused on retrofitting the existing academic buildings for improving the overall energy performance. Has been part of the foundation team of Architectural Colleges, wherein apart from taking care of academics looked after the administrative issues, affiliation procedures, conducting inspections, coordinating workshops, holding seminars, delivering add-on courses, etc. Prior to joining her Ph.D., she has worked in DLF projects limited as a Project Architect and was involved in design coordination and management of mixed-used commercial projects. She has also worked with Architect Jasbir Sawhney as an Architectural Consultant and worked on several projects including townships, commercial and institutional.

name: Dr. Seema Praliya
qualification: M.Arch. SPA Delhi, Ph.D. - IIT Roorkee
specialisation: Building Engineering and Management, Public Spaces
research_interests: Public Spaces
bio: Dr. Seema Praliya is an Assistant Professor in the School of Architecture and design. Dr. Praliya did her Ph.D. in Architecture from IIT Roorkee, She is a Master in Building Engineering and Management (M.B.E.M) and Bachelor of Architecture from School of Planning and Architecture, Delhi. Her interest areas are improving the condition of public spaces across Indian Cities, focusing on their management dimension. She has participated and presented in 4 international conferences and has published 2 national papers and 1 international paper in reputed journals.

name: Vaibhav Padhye
qualification: PhD (IIT BHU), M. Tech (BITS Pilani), B.E. (IES IPS Indore)
research_interests: Machine Learning, Reinforcement Learning, Recommender Systems
bio: He has obtained his B.E. degree from IES IPS Academy Indore and his master's degree from BITS Pilani. He has previous industry experience in 3DPLM Bengaluru as a Software Developer. His research areas include Reinforcement Learning and recommender systems.

name: Nainika Jaiswal
qualification: Master of Design Space, National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT)
specialisation: Strategy Design, UI/UX Design, Visual Communication
research_interests: Design Research, User Experience Design, Trend Analysis
bio: She holds a Master of Design Space from the National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT). During her postgraduate studies, she honed a comprehensive skill set covering the complete design lifecycle—from strategic research and trend analysis to UI/UX and visual communication. She is highly proficient in using industry-standard design and prototyping tools such as Figma, Adobe Illustrator, Adobe InDesign, and Google Analytics. She possesses a deep understanding of core design concepts, including Design Thinking, User Research, Strategy Design, and Generative AI tools.

name: Ashish Khanna
department: Chemistry as Assistant Professor in August 2023
qualification: M.Sc. (Kumaun University, Nainital) and Ph.D. (Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi)
research_interests: Carbohydrate Chemistry & Computational Chemistry.
bio: Dr. Ashish Khanna joined the Department of Chemistry as Assistant Professor in August 2023. He has received his B.Sc. (CBZ), and M.Sc. Chemistry degree from Kumaun University, Nainital and Ph.D. Chemistry degree from Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi, India under the supervision of Prof. Ram Sagar. His main research interest focuses on organic synthesis, the development of Carbohydrate derived bioactive molecules, natural product-inspired hybrid analogues and molecular modeling, especially in protein-ligand interaction via in-silico docking tools. He has attended many international and national conferences. He was awarded with GATE, USET and BHU-RET during his Ph.D. degree. He has published more than 11 research papers.

name: Karuna Evangeline Prakash
qualification: BBA from HNB Garhwal University, MBA from DIT University, PhD from DIT University
research_interests: Generational Studies, Organizational Psychology/Behaviour, Psychological Contracts, Sustainability at the workplace
bio: Ms.Karuna’s interests focus around organizational behaviour/psychology (psychological contracts, expectations), generational studies (millennials and gen-z), people management and general management. She has attended various workshops and FDP’s and has also presented papers in International conferences which are published in the proceedings of ICRBS2017 and ICSDSIB2019. She has contributed to research and has published three papers in SCOPUS listed International journals

name: Abhishek Bhardwaj
qualification: MA (Clinical Psychology), NET (Yoga), PhD
research_interests: Psycho-yogic interventions for mental health and psycho-immunity
bio: Dr. Bhardwaj completed his Post graduation and PhD in Clinical Psychology. Being an avid researcher in the field of Yoga/CAM and mental health, he served as Scientist-C at ‘Patanjali Research Foundation’, Haridwar from 2010 to 2017. Five scholars have been completed their doctoral research with his guidance. At present he is supervising three scholars in the area of yoga and mental health. Apart from this, he is a member of editorial and review board in many national/ international journals.

name: Dr. Chetan Kumar
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
department: School of Pharmaceutical & Populations Health Informatics as an Assistant Professor in July 2023
qualification: Ph.D. from CSIR-Indian Institute of Integrative Medicine Jammu, India.
research_interests: Phytochemistry, Medicinal Chemistry, Structural Modification, SAR development
bio: Dr. Chetan Kumar joined the Department of School of Pharmaceutical & Populations Health Informatics as an Assistant Professor in July 2023. Dr. Chetan received his M. Pharm. in Pharmaceutical Chemistry from Kumaun University Nainital, Uttarakhand, in 2013, and his PhD in Chemical sciences from the CSIR-Indian Institute of Integrative Medicine, Jammu, in 2023. He began working as a research fellow at CSIR-IIIM Jammu in 2014, and in 2019 he was awarded CSIR-Direct SRF fellowship for 3 years. Dr. Chetan has more than 10 years of research experience and has published in reputable journals more than 15 research articles, three book chapters, and one review article. He continues his work in the area of natural product chemistry (Extraction, isolation, characterization etc.) as well as structural modification of bioactive natural products for the enhancing the biological activity for drug discovery.

name: Dr. Mohd Habban Akhter
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
qualification: PhD, National Institute of Medical Science University (NIMS), Jaipur, India.
research_interests: Pharmaceutics, Drug Delivery, Nanomedicine, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics.
courses_taught: MPH203T (Computer aided drug Development) PH742 Targeted Drug Delivery system
bio: Dr Akhter is working as Assistant Professor-I in School of Pharmaceutical and Population Health Informatics (SoPPHI), DIT University, Dehradun, India since 09 October 2017. He has graduated and Master in Pharmacy (Pharmaceutics) from Jamia Hamdard, New Delhi and completed doctoral program in 2016. He had qualified GATE Exam conducted by IIT, Kanpur, Govt of India, percentile score 97.04 in 2007. He had honoured with JRF by UGC Govt. of India, SRF by DST-SERB Govt of India. His work of interest is novel drug delivery system, nanomedicine, nanotechnology-based formulation design, engineering nanoparticles, their surface functionalization for various target diseases including cancer treatment, and development of animal model/xenograft model. He has published >65 research and review papers in high impact journal of repute, 05 book chapters, 08 patents published and 03 Granted, and peer reviewed >61 articles. His present cumulative impact factor is >160 and H-index is 24, Google scholar citation is 1610, Scopus (Elsevier) citation (1,196), and Publon-Web of Science citation (1,125). He has attended and presented many papers at national and international conferences/seminars/symposia. He is an editorial board member of Eur J Med and Oncology (JCR Clarivate IF-2.5). He has been awarded with the research excellence award for the consecutive years 2019, and 2020, and 2021, and 2022 in DIT University.

name: Mandeep Kumar Arora
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
qualification: Ph. D in Pharmacy (Sept: 2014) from Swami Vivekanand Subharti University, Meerut, India.
research_interests: Diabetic Complications, Vascular Pharmacology, Neurodegenerative Disorders, Pharmacovigilance.
bio: Dr. Arora has also two years of International experience- (worked in Glasgow, United Kingdom in the field of nutrition, 2004-2006). He has been awarded with certificate for Highly cited research by Elsevier-Vascular Pharmacology (according to data from Scopus), and honoured by Indian Pharmacological Society for his services rendered in the capacity of Organizing Secretary for NRIPSCON-2017. Most recently, Dr. Arora was conferred upon best faculty award-2024 by the APTI, Uttrakhand. Dr. Arora also awarded with “Teachers of the Year-2021” award by the Honourable Chief Minister of Uttrakhand, Shri Pushkar Singh Dhami, on Teacher's day. He has published more than 10 books/book chapters; more than 10 patents; completed one Govt. funded project and having more than 35 publications in International Journals with high Impact factor with 1250 citations. Dr. Arora has organized various scientific programmes including Faculty Development Program, National/International Conferences, Seminars, Workshops etc. in the field of Pharmacology. Dr. Arora is also serving as CCSEA nominee. Before joining DIT, he was working as Associate Professor at KIET Group of Institutions, Ghaziabad.

name: Dr. Mahendra
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
department: Pharmaceutical Technology (Formulations)
qualification: Ph. D (Pharmaceutical Science) from NIPER, Mohali (SAS Nagar), Punjab
research_interests: Amorphous solid dispersions, Phospholipid complexes and Oral drug delivery
bio: Dr. Mahendra Singh had joined Faculty of Pharmacy, DIT University on 9th August 2022 in a capacity of Assistant Professor-III. Prior to this, he has worked in the position of Junior research fellow (CSIR Project, Principal Investigator- Dr. Sarasija Suresh), at Department of Pharmaceutical Technology (Formulations), National Institute of Pharmaceutical Education and Research, Mohali, India and Assistant Professor at Chandigarh college of pharmacy, Mohali, Punjab. He has total 4 years (3.5 -year teaching + 6-month research) of experience excluding PhD. Dr. Mahendra Singh completed his M. Pharm (2010-2012) and PhD (2013-2019) in Pharmaceutical Technology from the National Institute of Pharmaceutical Education and Research (NIPER), Mohali (Punjab), M. Pharm from NIPER, Mohali (Punjab) and B. Pharm from Babu Banarasi das National Institute of Technology and Management, Lucknow (U.P) affiliated to AKTU, Lucknow (U.P). His major research area includes: oral drug delivery systems, Amorphous solid dispersions, Phospholipid complex and drug polymer interactions.

name: Santosh Singh
qualification: Ph.D. from Gurukul Kangri Vishwavidyalaya
research_interests: Indian Folk Theatre, Street theatre, Village theatre, Third Theatre, Postcolonial studies
bio: Dr. Santosh Singh is an Assistant Professor in the School of Liberal Arts and Management. Dr Santosh Singh received his PhD in English Literature from Gurukul Kangri Vishwavidyalaya, Haridwar, and is a MA in English Literature and BA from HNB Garhwal University. He is having more than 22 years of experience in industry and teaching. His research interest lies in Indian Street theatre, Postcolonial Studies and Indian Writing in English. He has many research papers and book chapters in his credit in nationally and internationally reputed journals. He was an Assistant Professor at Raffles University Neemrana, Rajasthan and Uttaranchal University, Uttarakhand.

name: Priyanka Dahiya
qualification: M.Tech. Sikkim  Manipal University, PhD Manipal University, Jaipur
specialisation: Data Mining, Deep Learning, Machine Learning, Intrusion Detection and Big Data
research_interests: Information Security, Big Data Analytics, Data science
bio: Priyanka Dahiya joined School of Computing as an Assistant Professor in August 2018. Her Research group is data science in SOC. Priyanka received her Ph.D. from Manipal University Jaipur, Rajasthan, India, and Master’s degree from Sikkim Manipal Institute of Technology, Sikkim, India, in Computer Science and Engineering. She is having more than 10 years of experience in the organization like Manipal University Jaipur and Mody University. Priyanka current research interests include data mining, deep learning, intrusion detection, cryptography and big data. She has published various patents, research papers, and invited as session chair in reputed conferences. Priyanka is certified as Publons Academy Supervisor.

name: Ravi Kumar Shukla
department: Physics
qualification: M.Sc. & M. Tech Barkatullah university Bhopal, PhD, Thapar University,  Post Doctoral, Technical University Darmstadt, Germany
research_interests: Soft matters, Colloidal and emulsion systems  Ferroelectric Liquid crystalline materials , Lyotropic Liquid crystalline materials, Nano material (soft templated nano structured materials), Green lyotropic liquid crystals,Graphene based Liquid crystals , Carbon and its allotropes, Purification studies,  Polymer nanocomposites
courses_taught: Wave Optics and Introduction to Quantum Mechanics
bio: Dr. Ravi Kumar Shukla joined Department of Physics, School of Physics Sciences DIT University in 2014. He has done his PhD in Physics from Thapar university, Patiala, Punjab and Post Doctorate from Technical University Darmstadt, Germany. Dr. Shukla is involved in teaching, research and many administrative responsibilities (Associate Head Department of Physics). He is teaching various courses to Ph.D., M.Sc., B.Sc. and B. Tech. level. Courses undertaken by Dr. Shukla are Nanoscience and Nanotechnology, Nanomaterials and their applications, Materials Characterization, Soft matter physics, Statistical Mechanics, Renewal Energy and Energy Harvesting, Device Application and Instrumentation, Engineering Physics, Wave Optics and Introduction to Quantum Mechanics. On research front, Dr. Shukla is actively working in the field of soft materials, nanocolloids, nanostructured materials, carbon-based materials, graphene based liquid crystals, dye degradation and adsorption and water purification technologies, polymers and polymer nanocomposites, antibacterial activities of liquid crystals and their hybrids. Dr. Shukla has expertise in the synthesis of liquid crystals, nanomaterials, colloids and polymer-based materials/ thin films and familiar with characterizations techniques Viz. XRD, SEM, UV-Vis, FTIR, POM, dielectric, optical and electrooptical.

name: Manisha Duseja
department: Chemistry
qualification: M.Sc., Ph.D (G.B Pant University of Agriculture and Technology, Pantnagar)
specialisation: Natural Product Chemistry, Medicinal Chemistry, Or
research_interests: Natural Product Chemistry, Medicinal Chemistry, Organic Chemistry
bio: Dr. Manisha Duseja joined the Department of Chemistry, DIT University, Dehradun as Assistant Professor in February 2010. She received her M.Sc and Ph.D degree in Chemistry from G.B Pant University of Agriculture & Technology, Pantnagar. Dr Manisha’s area of research is Phytochemistry & Pharmacognosy (Natural Product Chemistry). It is particularly related to extraction & isolation of natural products from plants indigenous to the Indian subcontinent; unravel their structure by spectroscopic procedures and determination of their pharmacological activities. She has various research publications in journals of repute, attended and organized several national and international conferences. During her long association with DIT University she has taught various subjects of undergraduate, post graduate and PhD programmes and has been an integral part of various committees in the Department of Chemistry. Along with the academics she has also taken up several other responsibilities such as she has been chairperson of Social and Cultural Committee as well as an overall coordinator of all the student clubs in the University.

name: Jyotsna Dogra
department: Computer Science
qualification: M.Tech.- JIIT Noida, Ph.D. – JUIT (Wakhnaghat), Post-Doctoral Fellow - IIT Guwahati
research_interests: Image Analysis, Machine Learning
bio: Dr. Jyotsna Dogra is an Assistant Professor in the School of Computing in the Department of Computer Science. She was a postdoctoral fellow at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Guwahati for two years from December 2020-2022. She received her PhD in biomedical image analysis from Jaypee University of Information Technology (JUIT) Wakhnaghat in 2020, and her M.Tech in Electronics and Communication from Jaypee Institute of Information Technology (JIIT) Noida in 2013.

name: Gopal Kumar
qualification: M.Tech.  ISM Dhanbad ,Ph.D. (Pursuing) DIT University
research_interests: Geologic Carbon Storage, Waste to Wealth
bio: His current research focuses on developing novel materials from waste for various engineering applications. Mr. Kumar has published numerous research articles in reputable journals and has presented his work at various national and international conferences. For his extraordinary contributions to furthering knowledge via both his research and his dedication to educating and mentoring students, Mr. Kumar has received awards, including Excellence in Research and Best Paper award. Mr. Kumar is an active member of the Society of Petroleum Engineers (SPE). In addition to his membership, he serves as the Faculty Advisor of the DIT SPE Student Chapter since 2017. The chapter, under his guidance, recently received the prestigious Presidential Award for Outstanding Student Chapter 2023 from SPE. Recognizing his expertise and dedication to innovation, Mr. Kumar has been appointed as a certified Innovation Ambassador by the Ministry of Education. Furthermore, he holds a patent for his invention titled "Amine grafted agricultural waste-based aerogel with in-situ hydrophobic characteristic for CO2 capture." Mr. Gopal Kumar is a respected academician who is influencing the direction of technical education in petroleum engineering through his broad knowledge, contributions to research, teaching talent, and dedication to student development.

name: Naresh Mohan Chadha
department: Mathematics
qualification: MSc from IIT Roorkee. PhD from University of Limerick, Ireland. Postdoc from National University of Ireland, Galway, Ireland
research_interests: Scientific Computing, Applied Mathematics, Mathematical modeling, Non-linear differential Equations from Plasma Physics, Adaptive mesh generation, Singularly perturbed Differential Equations and their numerical treatment.
bio: Dr. Naresh M. Chadha is working as Dean, School of Physical Sciences, and Professor & Head in Department of Mathematics. He earned his PhD from Department of Mathematics & Statistics, University of Limerick, Limerick, Ireland; Postdoc from National University of Ireland, Galway, Ireland. He is recipient of prestigious Royal Irish Fellowship award and he has more than more 22 years of experience in teaching mathematics both in India and abroad. His research topics include (1) Adaptive mesh generation, (2) Devising novel algorithms for advection dominated problems, (3) Mesh free collocation methods, (4) Scientific Computing and mathematical modeling of real world problems. He has published several scientific research papers in the leading peer-reviewed journals in his area, and have participated in various international conferences. Recently, he published a book titled PROGRAMMING IN MATLAB WITH APPLIED NUMERICAL METHODS FOR ENGINEERS AND SCIENTISTS. The book is available in more than 130 countries via Amazon platform.

name: Dr. Sanjoli Arya
qualification: BPT – Swami Rama Himalayan University, MPT (Musculoskeletal) – Swami Rama Himalayan University
research_interests: Musculoskeletal Disorders, Advanced Techniques
bio: In recognition of her academic excellence, she was awarded the Gold Medal by the Governor of Uttarakhand for securing the highest percentage and distinction in her MPT program. Her research, titled “Analysing Immediate and Carryover Effect of Primal Reflex Release Technique on Iliotibial Band Tightness in Knee Pain Patients”, was published in the Indian Journal of Applied Research (IJAR), indexed in Google Scholar, and holds registered copyright.

name: Dr. Pratiti Haldar
qualification: Ph.D. (Nursing Sciences), PGDEMA, CNCC, M.Sc. (N)
research_interests: Child Health, Child Nutrition, Evidenced based Nursing, New born Care practices, Adolescent Health, Management
bio: A committed faculty with over 12 years of experience in clinical as well as teaching institutions. In-depth knowledge of various techniques and approaches applied at a research project. His research area is distinguished by a multidisciplinary, evidence-based approach to exploring yoga and mind-body practices in relation to psychological well-being, physiological functioning, cognitive enhancement, and community health promotion. He contributes to academic governance as a member of the Board of Studies (BoS) and participates in Examination and Evaluation Committees across several universities. He also serves as a reviewer of various national and international journals. He is Co-Editor of the book Evidence-Based Perspectives on the Psychophysiology of Yoga and Its Applications, as well as a special issue of Frontiers in Bioscience: The Science of Mind-body Practices. He has authored over 60 research papers, book chapters, and evidence-based articles in leading peer-reviewed journals, including those indexed by PubMed, Medline, Scopus, Web of Science, and PsycINFO, with more than 773 citations to his work.

bio: Her research area includes identification of coding sequence from genomic DNA sequence. Therefore, both approaches – Conventional as well as Computational Intelligences (CI) have been pursued to predict the splice site in DNA sequence of the Eukaryotic organism and, both have been evaluated and compared in terms of their performance. We use computational techniques and statistical technique to work on diseased data for disease and psychological disorder prediction. He also qualified UGC-JRF & NET for Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) in 2011 and National Eligibility Test (NET) for Lectureship in 2009. He has gained various certification courses from various platforms such as Edx, Coursera, Microsoft and NPTEL. His research involves several dimensions in the field of Strategic Human Resource Management, Stress Management, Organizational Development, Change Management and Workplace spirituality. He has published several research papers in UGC listed and Scopus indexed journals repute. He has presented his research work at national and international conferences such as IIMs, IITs, Goa University, MDI and BHU etc. He has a good command of research tools of social sciences such as SPSS, PROCESS macro, SMART PLS, R language etc. Apart from that, he has developed Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs) at the UDEMY platform. Goenka University Gurugram Haryana, India. She is a CBT (cognitive behavioural therapy) practioner since last 9 years. Her research lies at the intersections of climate change, mental health and climate anxiety. Her overall research objective is to characterize interventions that address climate and environmental vulnerability to improve the health and well-being of overall population. Throughout her academic journey, she has contributed to various scholarly articles, book chapters and presented her findings at numerous conferences, earning recognition for her innovative approach to tackling climate-related mental health issues specifically climate anxiety. Through her teaching, she is committed to educating the next generation of psychologists, fostering a supportive and engaging learning environment that encourages critical thinking and a deep understanding of the complexities of psychology.

bio: Debashish Chowdhury joined the Department of Physics as a Professor in July 2023. Before joining he was a Professor (HAG) of Physics at IIT Kanpur. In recognition of his outstanding research achievements, he has been elected as a fellow of the Indian National Science Academy (Delhi), Indian Academy of Sciences (Bangalore), and National Academy of Sciences India (Allahabad). Her research interests extend to feminist theories, cultural studies, and film narratives, focusing on how these frameworks shape understandings of gender and embodiment. She has experience teaching English Literature and Communication Skills and has served as a peer reviewer for reputed academic journals. Her publications include journal articles, book chapters, and review articles in high-impact, peer-reviewed journals.

He has joined as an assistant professor in January 2015. After completed his doctorate in chemistry from IIT Madras in 2011 and a Postdoctoral Fellowship at CWRU Cleveland, Ohio, USA (2011–2014). He has also been honored with ‘Best Thesis Award’ in PhD Chemistry [Prof. C. N. Pillai Prize for Organic & Biochemistry] by IIT Madras in 2012. During his doctoral and postdoctoral study, he has published many research articles in peer-reviewed journals. He also supervised many Masters and Ph.D. scholars at DIT University. His area of research interest includes green additives for fuels and lubricants, Biolubricant Formulations, Biopolymers, Synthetic Methodologies, Natural Products Chemistry and Synthesis of hydrophilic and hydrophobic Aerogels for engineering applications. Dr. Porwal's significant contributions to education and research culminated in his recognition by the state of Uttarakhand as the recipient of the "Excellence in Research of the Year 2022" award.

qualification: M.Des NID, B.Tech Tezpur University
research_interests: Play Centric Experiences, Game Based Learning, Coputational Game Design Systems for Emergent Gameplay
bio: Bhaskarjyoti Bora joined the School of Design at DIT University as an Assistant Professor II in February 2026. He holds a Master’s degree in Digital Game Design from the National Institute of Design and a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science and Engineering from Tezpur University. Bora brings substantial professional experience as a game designer and gamification designer, with expertise spanning digital games, game-based learning, and play-centric interactive systems.

name: Dr. Ashok Behera
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
qualification: PhD (Pharmacy), Post Doc (CSIR), MRSC.
specialisation: Medicinal chemistry, Drug delivery and CADD
research_interests: Peptidomimetics and Peptide conjugates as therapeutics and drug delivery, Receptor specific targeted drug delivery, Computer aided drug design, Organic synthesis and purification, Analytical techniques and structure elucidation and Molecular imaging
bio: Dr. Ashok Behera had joined Faculty of Pharmacy, DIT, University on 11th Jan 2019 in a capacity of Assistant Professor. Prior to this, he has worked in the position Postdoctoral Research Associateship of (Council of Scientific & Industrial Research (CSIR), An Autonomous Body of Govt of India) 2015-2018, and Assistant professor at the Pharmacy Department, The MS University of Baroda, Vadodara, Gujarat (2013-15). He has total 14 year of mixed experience including teaching, and research. Dr. Behera completed his PhD (Pharmacy) in June, 2014 from Jadavpur University (CSIR-Indian Institute of Chemical Biology), M.S Pharm in Medicinal chemistry 2009 from NIPER- KOLKATA and B. Pharm 2007 from BPUT, Odisha. He is recipient of Science and Engineering Research Board-Teachers Associateship For Research Excellence (SERB-TARE) fellowship 2020-24, Mentor for CSIR-summer research training program (CSIR-SRTP) 2020, Best E-poster presentation APTICON2019, ACS Chemical Biology Best poster award 2016, Post Doc Research Associateship (RA-CSIR) 2015-18, Best poster presentation of NIPICON-2014, Senior Research Fellowship (SRF-CSIR)2013, Young Researcher Award ARCEB2010, Senior Research Fellowship (SRF-DAE) 2009-12, GATE 2007 & 2009 (Graduate Aptitude Test for Engineers). He is also the principal investigator for several Govt and privately funded project. To his credit, he has published more than 20 peer-reviewed papers and 4 published patents. His major research includes, target-oriented and lead-based design and development of New Chemical Entities (NCEs) from natural and synthetic scaffolds against Anti-cancer, Antidiabetic, Antioxidant, Alzheimer’s and Hepatoprotective. Additionally, he is a member of the royal society of chemistry (MRSC), London, also serving as a life member of Indian peptide society. He is Currently guiding 3-PhD students and already guided 14 students for PG research projects.

name: Prateek Vishwakarma
qualification: M.Des. from UPES, B.E. for VTU
research_interests: Usability and Human Factors in Smart Products, Heutagogy and Self-Directed Learning in Design Education, User Experience in Iot Driven Systems, Cognitive Load and Decision Making in Complex Interfaces
bio: His academic and research interests focus on the convergence of engineering-led design, UX research, interaction design, and emerging technologies such as AI-driven interfaces, IoT-enabled products, data-informed user experiences, and intelligent systems. He actively explores how technology, cognition, and user behavior shape meaningful product and service experiences. Through studio-led pedagogy and research-informed instruction, he is committed to developing future-ready designers capable of designing scalable, intelligent, and human-centered solutions for complex real-world contexts.

name: Dr. Kapil Lakhera
qualification: MA in Economics from HNB Garhwal University, PhD from HNB Garhwal  University
research_interests: Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Rural Development
bio: Kapil Lakhera completed his PhD from HNB Garhwal University in 2024. The major areas of his teaching and research are Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Rural Development and Entrepreneurship. He has contributed as a research assistant in the Government of India, Ministry of Rural Development, RURBAN Mission project entitled “Third Party Evaluation Study of 100 Cluster of Phase I under Shyama Prasad Mukherjee RURBAN Mission”. He has published four research publications in national journals, attended three workshops, one FDP and presented two papers in national conferences.

name: Samta Manori
department: Physics at DIT University as an Assistant Professor in June 2016
qualification: M.Sc.  University of Delhi, M.Tech NIT Kurukshetra, PhD  IIT Roorkee
research_interests: Experimental Condensed Matter Physics: Multiferroics, Thin films, Magnetic nanomaterials, Double perovskites, Solid Oxide Fuel Cell
bio: Dr. Samta Manori joined the Department of Physics at DIT University as an Assistant Professor in June 2016. She received her PhD in Physics from Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee in 2016, and M.Sc in Physics with specialization in Electronics and B.Sc (Hons) Physics from University of Delhi. She also holds a degree in M.Tech (Instrumentation) from National Institute of Technology Kurukshetra. She has qualified CSIR-UGC NET in 2010 and GATE in 2008 and 2010. Her research interests involves the study of various phenomena of magnetism like exchange bias, spin glasses, disorders in nanostructures, interfacial coupling at nanoscale in thin films and nanoparticles. Presently, she is working on the development of thin films of double perovskite materials for applications in Solid Oxide fuel Cells. She is also been involved in a collaborative work on hydrogen sensors and optical coatings. Her research findings have applications in the field of spin valves and memory devices, sensors, actuators and many more.

name: Manamohan Tripathy
department: Chemistry
qualification: PhD (NIT Rourkela), Post-doctorate (IISc Bangalore and IIT(ISM) Dhanbad)
specialisation: Material and Environmental Chemistry, Physical Chemistry
research_interests: Biopolymer-based hybrid materials; electrospun nanofibers membrane; magnetic nanocomposites; MOF-based materials; removal of aquatic pollutants (organic and inorganic) by adsorption and photocatalytic degradation; superhydrophobic coatings on natural fabrics for oil-water separation and sustainable packaaging applications.
bio: Dr. Manamohan Tripathy joined the Department of Chemistry, DIT University, as an Assistant Professor in August 2025. He earned his Ph.D. in Chemistry with a specialization in Material and Environmental Chemistry from NIT Rourkela and has over two years of postdoctoral research experience at premier institutes, including IISc Bangalore and IIT(ISM) Dhanbad. His research focuses on developing nanohybrid, polymer-based, and bio-based materials for water and wastewater treatment, with expertise in adsorption and photocatalysis for pollutant remediation. He has also worked on biodegradable superhydrophobic coatings on jute fabrics for oil–water separation and sustainable packaging. Aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals, his work seeks to advance water quality, public health, and environmental sustainability. To date, Dr. Tripathy has published 12 research papers and 6 book chapters in reputed international journals and books.

name: Mrs. Dayabati Soyam
department: Nursing as a Professor on 12th August 2025
qualification: M.Sc Nursing
research_interests: Obstetric & Gynaecological Nursing, Medical & Surgical Nursing
bio: Dayabati Soyam, joined the Department of Nursing as a Professor on 12th August 2025. With more than 16 years of teaching and research experience. M.Sc Nursing, Bangalore City College of Nursing, Karnataka in 2013. I have contributed significantly to nursing education, student mentoring, student development and professional development. Dayabati has been actively involved in academic leadership, research projects, and professional training programs, and also organised and participated in national and international conferences. Areas of interest include nursing research, clinical teaching, competency-based education, and community health initiatives.

name: Rajeev Kumar Sharma
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
qualification: PhD, Uttarakhand Technical University
research_interests: Colon Targeted drug delivery, Synthetic Chemistry
bio: Dr. Rajeev Kumar Sharma is presently working as Associate Professor in the Faculty of Pharmacy, DIT University. He joined the University on 15th July 2006 as Lecturer and was subsequently promoted as Senior Lecturer in 2010, Assistant Professor-II in 2011, Assistant Professor-I in 2021, and later to his current position. He has more than 20 years of teaching and research experience. He obtained his Ph.D. in Pharmaceutical Sciences from Uttarakhand Technical University in 2015 under the supervision of Prof. (Dr.) A.K. Sharma, Ex-Vice Chancellor, SBS University, Ex-Principal, Eritrea Pharmacy College, Govt. of Eritrea, Asmara, and Ex-HOD, BITS Ranchi. He completed his M.Pharm. in Pharmaceutical Chemistry from Bundelkhand University, Jhansi in 2006, and his B.Pharm. from H.N.B. Garhwal University in 2001. He qualified GATE in 2003. His research interests include colon-targeted drug delivery and synthetic medicinal chemistry, and he is recognized as an expert in the field of synthetic chemistry.

name: Saurabh Mishra
qualification: M.Tech. Graphic Era University, Ph.D. (Pursuing)
specialisation: Electronic Circuit Designing, Wireless Sensor Netw
research_interests: Wireless Sensor Networks, Analog Circuits
bio: Mr. Saurabh Mishra is an Assistant Professor in the School of Engineering and Technology. He has completed his Bachelor of Technology from U.P. Technical University in Electronics and Communication Engineering and holds a Master of Technology in Microwave and Mobile Communications from G.E.U. Dehradun . He has submitted his thesis for Ph.D. in 2018 to Uttarakhand Technical University Dehradun in the Wireless Sensor Networks. Having 14 research articles published in journals of repute. He is the member of SPIE and OSA and has served as Vice-president of SPIE-DITU student chapter for one year.

name: Vikash Kumar Mishra
qualification: M.A. V.B.S Purvanchal University, Ph.D. Banaras Hindu University
research_interests: International Economics, Development Economics
bio: Dr. Mishra is currently working as an Assistant Professor of Economics, at the School of Liberal Arts and Management, DIT University, Dehradun. He has over 6 years of rich experience in academia. He has earned his Ph.D. in Economics from Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi. His research work entitled ‘Indo-German Trade Relations Since 1990’ has been published in Germany. He has completed his M.A. in Economics from Veer Bahadur Singh Purvanchal University, Jaunpur.

name: Rajni Goel
department: Mathematics at DIT University
qualification: M.Sc., MPhil, PhD, Chaudhary Charan Singh University Campus Meerut, India
specialisation: Censored data analysis, Statistical Inference, Statistical Simulation, Reliability Analysis.
research_interests: Different kinds of censoring schemes include the joint censoring scheme, the adaptive censoring scheme, the progressive censoring scheme, etc. Bayesian and classical analysis of complete and censored data
bio: Dr. Rajni Goel joined the Department of Mathematics at DIT University, Dehradun, as an Assistant Professor in August 2024. She earned her Ph.D. from Chaudhary Charan Singh University in 2021. Her research interests include censored data analysis, classical inference, Bayesian inference, statistical simulation, and reliability analysis. Dr. Goel has over four years of experience teaching both undergraduate and postgraduate classes, along with six years of research experience. She has also guided postgraduate students. She has published 12 research papers in prestigious international journals and conferences, with publications in well-known publishers such as Springer, Taylor & Francis, and AIP. Additionally, she has actively participated in numerous international conferences.

name: Natasha Awasthi
qualification: M.Sc. Kumaun University, Ph.D. Govind Ballabh Pant University of Agriculture & Technology
research_interests: I am a theoretical Physicist, currently working in open quantum system, quantum information theory and quantum communication
bio: I am a theoretical Physicist , currently working in the field of quantum mechanics basically an open quantum system. Currently, I am working in the field of quantum information theory as well as quantum communication. Moreover, I am open to work in the filed of quantum field theory as well as collapse system. I did my PhD from GB pant University, where I worked with my guide under the collaboration of Professor Ujjwal Sen and Aditi Sen De and we derive an uncertainty relation in the filed of open quantum system and my published my first word in Physical Review A (2018).

name: Dr. Pradipta Bhattacharyya
qualification: BOPTM, MPhil, PhD
research_interests: Corneal anatomy and physiology, advanced contact lenses
bio: My journey began with a bachelor's in optometry from Vidyasagar College of Optometry and Vision Science. I completed an M.Phil. from Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani, and earned my Ph.D. from the Queensland University of Technology, Brisbane, where I made significant research contributions. Notably, my doctoral research focused on the in vivo examination of the corneal epithelium, offering valuable insights into corneal health in complex ocular surface diseases and environmental factors such as exposure to ultraviolet radiation from the Sun. After completing my Ph.D. I joined the University of Houston, College of Optometry as a post-doctoral research fellow where I conducted research on ocular immunology. I presented my research in various national and international scientific forums and published research papers in peer-reviewed journals.

name: Mony Singh
department: Humanities & Liberal Arts as an Assistant Professor in January 2024
qualification: M.A psychology, PG Diploma in Counselling & Communication, PhD from Gurukul Kangri (Deemed to be University)
research_interests: Developmental Psychology, Social Psychology
bio: Mony Singh joined the Department of Humanities & Liberal Arts as an Assistant Professor in January 2024. Mony received his PhD in Psychology from Gurukul Kangri Deemed to be University, and an MA in Psychology from Indira Gandhi national Open University (IGNOU). Mony’s interests centre upon developmental psychology especially in the area of disability. She has published research paper in UGC-Care listed journal and peer-reviewed journal. She has presented several research papers in national and international conferences and attended seminars or workshops related with research methodology.

name: Dr. Satish Shilpi
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
department: Pharmaceutical Sciences
qualification: Ph. D., Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences, Dr. H. S. Gour University, Sagar (MP), India
research_interests: Targeted Drug Delivery Nanotechnology, Novel Drug Delivery Systems, Cancer Cell Targeting, Drug Delivery for Arthritis and Diabetes Treatment, Treatment of Multi Dug Resistance Cancer
bio: I am Dr. Satish Shilpi completed bachelor, master's degree in pharmacy from the Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences, Dr. H. S. Gour Vishwavidyalaya, Sagar, MP India in 2004 and 2007 respectively, which is one of prestigious and oldest pharmacy institute in India. I did my doctorate degree from Dr. H. S. Gour Vishwavidyalaya, Sagar, MP India in 2014. Currently I am working as Assistant Professor-I in School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics, DIT University, Dehradun, Uttarakhand from September 2022. Additional I got the responsibility of Faculty Warden and Proctor in DIT University. I have more than 20 Year of experience in teaching and research in different reputed institutions in which 10 years of teaching and research experience after Ph.D. degree. After joining the DIT University, I published 19 research/review and book chapters, 02 Indian patents, and one authored book. In my credential, I published more than 67 research and review papers having 1380 citation with h-index 17, and i10-index 20 in high impact internationally reputed journals. I published 01 authored book and contributed more than 20 book chapters in the leading international books with reputed publishers like Elsevier, Springer Science, Bentham Science and others. I have 05 Indian patent publications. Previously I worked as an Associate Professor and Research Coordinator in Amity Institute of Pharmacy, Amity University Madhya Pradesh, Gwalior in year 2022. I contributed substantially to research on novel and target drug delivery, involving polymeric and lipid nano drug carriers modified for tumor cell targeting. I completed two major research projects with 40 lakh of research grant which was awarded from SERB-DST, New Delhi and MP Council of Science and Technology, Bhopal. I got BRG grant from Bioencapsulation Research Group, France in 2011. I got BioAsia Innovation award 2013, Techno Srijan Award 2020. Currently I awarded with SEED Money Research Grant from DIT University Dehradun in support my research. My research areas of interest include applications of nanotechnological principles in drug delivery, drug targeting through non-invasive methods and oral delivery of macromolecules such as monoclonal antibodies, protein and peptide and anticancer drugs.

name: Pankaj Kumar Pal
qualification: M.Sc. Animation & Multimedia from Singhania University,    MBA (IT & Marketing) from Shobhit University.
research_interests: Visual Graphics, Animation & Design
bio: His academic journey started at Reliance Education, followed by significant teaching experience at Arena Animation & Gaming. With a strong foundation in both industry practice and education, he brings a balanced, hands-on approach to teaching animation and design. Pankaj Pal is deeply passionate about transforming imagination into visual narratives and continues to explore and evolve within the ever-changing animation industry.

name: Diwaker
department: Computer Science & Engineering as an Assistant Professor in July 2019
qualification: M.E NITTTR Chandigarh, Ph.D.(pursuing) DIT University, Dehradun
specialisation: Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning and Robotics
research_interests: Machine Learning
courses_taught: Programming and problem solving in C
bio: Diwaker, joined the School of Computing, Department of Computer Science & Engineering as an Assistant Professor in July 2019. Diwaker received his M.Tech in CSE from NITTTR Chandigarh, Panjab University, and B.Tech in Computer Science & Engineering from UPTU Lucknow. Diwaker’s interests center upon Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Artificial Neural Networks and Remote Sensing, particularly Big Data Analytics. His research have been published in Journal of Indian Patents, Book Chapters in Springer Nature and Proceedings of IEEE Explore, among others. After joining DIT University, Diwaker is pursuing PhD from DIT University, itself.

name: Shulanki Pal
department: Civil Engineering as an Assistant Professor in March 2023
qualification: PhD from NIT Silchar
research_interests: Disaster management, Structural health safety, Retrofitting and Rehabilitation of Structure
bio: Dr. Shulanki Pal joined the Department of Civil Engineering as an Assistant Professor in March 2023. Dr. Pal received her PhD in Civil Engineering from NIT Silchar, and M.Tech degree in Structural Engineering and B.Tech Degree in Civil Engineering from NIT Agartala. Her interests center upon Dynamic Response Mitigation, Structural health safety, Retrofitting and Rehabilitation of Structure. Dr. Pal has published several number of papers in various reputed Journals. She is the winner of the Best Paper Award at ICRDSI ’19 for paper “Comparison of effectiveness of TLCBD over LCVA in vibration control of structure under non-stationary earthquake”; her conference papers have been published in the Proceedings of ICRDSI ’19, Proceedings of ASMA ‘21, and Proceedings of ACMS ’18, with springer publication. Aided to the above she is also a certified Breath and Meditation teacher and volunteer for several social awareness and Youth empowerment courses.

name: Shriya Goyal
department: Humanities & Liberal Arts as Associate Professor in October 2021
qualification: M.A. D.Phil.
research_interests: Indian Writing in English
bio: Dr. Shriya Goyal joined Department of Humanities & Liberal Arts as Associate Professor in October 2021. She received her D.Phil. in English from H.N.B. Garhwal University in the year 2006. She has over 18 years of experience comprising of teaching at Postgraduate and Undergraduate level and research experience. Dr. Shriya Goyal has supervised many Ph.D. Scholars. She has presented Research Papers and attended National and International Conferences, Seminars & Workshops. She has published articles in International Journals, including Scopus-indexed journals, to her credit. She has performed the duties assigned as Selection Committee Expert for Appointments (UTU), Expert Member of RDC (UTU), Additional Senior Superintendent (Examinations) of a Centre (HNBGU), Appointed Examiner (HNBGU & UTU) and Head & Deputy Head (Evaluation) of the assigned Centers (UTU). She has been in the jury for many prestigious Debate Competitions organized by reputed schools and colleges of the city.

name: Uddipak Rai
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
qualification: PhD (IIT-BHU, Varanasi)
research_interests: Diabetes, Insulin resistance, Insulin signaling pathways, Diabetic nephropathy, Inflammation, Gastric ulcer, Cancer, Molecular pharmacological techniques
courses_taught: B. Pharm: Human Anatomy and Physiology-I (BP101T)
M. Pharm (Pharmacology): Cellular and Molecular Pharmacology (MPL104T)
bio: Dr. Uddipak Rai joined the School of Pharmaceutical and Population Health Informatics as an Assistant Professor (Pharmacology) in July 2021. Dr. Rai received his PhD in Pharmacology from IIT-BHU, Varanasi, U.P. in the year 2020. He had completed his M. Pharm in Pharmacology from BIT-Mesra, Ranchi, Jharkhand in 2013 and B. Pharm from SRMS College of Engineering and Technology, Bareilly, U.P. in the year 2011. Earlier, before joining DIT University he worked as an Assistant Professor in the BBD University, Lucknow, U.P. and SRMS College of Engineering and Technology, Bareilly, U.P. He is having total research and teaching experience of 12 years after completion of post graduation. Dr. Rai’s interests center upon type-2 diabetes, diabetic nephropathy, insulin signaling pathways, inflammation, gastric ulcer, cancer and molecular pharmacological techniques such as western blotting and real-time PCR. He is having total 21 quality publications in the reputed and indexed journals with total 470 citations (h-index: 8; i10-index: 8). He is also a life-time member of Indian Pharmacological Society (IPS) and Association of Pharmaceutical Teachers of India (APTI). Currently he is supervising four PhD students. He had also supervised seven M. Pharm students and currently supervising two M. Pharm students.

name: Dr. Anglina Kisku
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
department: Pharmacy
qualification: PhD from IGNT University, Amarkantak (M.P.) and M. Pharm from KIET School of Pharmacy, Ghaziabad (U.P.).
research_interests: Neurosciences, Nanoformulations and Animal behavioral assessment
bio: Anglina Kisku joined the Faculty of Pharmacy as an Assistant Professor in September 2024. Anglina has completed PhD in Pharmacy from IGNT University, Amarkantak, and an M. Pharm in Pharmacology from KIET School of Pharmacy, Ghaziabad and a B.Pharm from MJP Rohilkhand University, Bareilly. Anglina’s interests centre on neuropsychiatric disorders and their management using traditional medicines and advancement in drug delivery systems. She has received travel grants for ISN-MLSU First Neurochemistry School on Advances in Neurochemical Research Techniques & Management of Neurological Disorders; received Best Poster Award in International Conference (ICNAND2023) sponsored by IBRO and organized by the Department of Pharmacy, IGNTU, Amarkantak, MP, India; her conference papers have been published in the proceedings of international symposiums and IBRO international conferences. Secured second position in Dream project entitled “Role of millets in treating maternal immune stress induced autism spectrum disorder in mice” in DST-STUTI sponsored training program on ‘’Biomedical Research Tools and Techniques” organised by IGNTU, Amarkantak.

name: Digant Sharma
qualification: Masters in Interior Design
research_interests: Human Centric Design/ Design for specially abled/ Sustainable Design/ Emotional Design.
bio: Digant Sharma joined School of Design as Associate Professor in September 2024. Digant received the Masters in Design degree from Punjab Technical University specializing in Interior Design, The Bachelors degree in Architectural Science specializing in Interior Design from BIT Mesra Ranchi. He comes with a rich teaching experience of 24 years working with reputed institutions & universities like Apeejay Institute of Design and Amity School of Design (Amity University, Noida) Prior to getting into the teaching he as accuired industry experience where he as worked in architectural/design firms and also practiced the consultancy and execution of variety of projects ranging from residential/commercial/retail.

name: Reetika Sehgal
department: Management Studies
qualification: PhD from BIT Mesra, Ranchi
research_interests: Human Resource Management, Organizational Behaviour, Consumer Behaviour, General Management and Public Administration
courses_taught: Social And Industrial Psychology and Business Communication
bio: Dr. Reetika Sehgal is working as Assistant Professor (HR/OB) in the Department of Management Studies, DIT University, Dehradun, since January 2025. She holds a Ph.D. in Management from Birla Institute of Technology, Mesra, Ranchi. Her research area includes Human Resource Management, Organizational Behavior, Consumer Behavior, General Management and Public Administration. She has over 12 years of teaching experience at graduate and post-graduate levels. She has published several research papers in international and national journals. She has presented her research work at national and international conferences such as IIMs, IITs, NITs etc. She has gained various certification courses from National Program on Technology Enhanced Learning (NPTEL). She has been a reviewer for various national and international journals. She has been honored with the award “Academic Excellence” and “Nation Builder Award” for exemplary contribution towards the betterment of educational objectives.

name: Abhishek Kumar
department: Petroleum Engineering as an Assistant Professor in August 2024
qualification: M.Tech RGIPT Raebareli, PhD (pursuing) from IIT Madras
research_interests: Reservoir Simulation, Shale Gas Reservoirs, Fractured Dual-Porosity Reservoirs, Numerical Modeling of Nonlinear Flow in Porous Media
bio: Mr. Abhishek Kumar joined the Department of Petroleum Engineering as an Assistant Professor in August 2024. With a rich blend of academia and practical experience spanning over two years, Abhishek is currently pursuing his PhD in Petroleum Engineering from IIT Madras. His educational journey also includes an MTech in Petroleum Engineering from RGIPT Raebareli and a BTech in Applied Petroleum Engineering from UPES Dehradun.

name: Pradeep Singh Rawat
department: Computer Science & Engineering as an Assistant Professor on 7 January 2010
qualification: M.Tech. (UTU), Ph.D. (Thesis Submitted) Uttrakhand Technical University
specialisation: Cloud Computing & Its Application, Data Communication & Networking
research_interests: Cloud Computing & Its Application, Data Communication & Networking, Data Science
bio: Pradeep Singh Rawat Joined the Department of Computer Science & Engineering as an Assistant Professor on 7 January 2010. He is doing his Ph.D. work in Computer Science & Engineering from Uttarakhand Technical University, Dehradun, India. He has submitted his Ph.D. Thesis on 15 January 2021. He received his MTech in Information Security & Management from Uttarakhand Technical University, Dehradun, and BTech in Computer Science & Engineering from Kumaun University, Nainital. Pradeep’s interests center upon Cloud Computing & its Application, Data Communication & Networking, Data Science Application in Cloud, and Soft Computing. He received the Research Excellence Award 2020-21 at DITU and is a Bronze medallist in his post-graduation (MTech) from Uttarakhand Technical University, Dehradun, India. He has published four papers in SCIE indexed Journal with the highest impact factor (5.472). Mr. Pradeep Singh Rawat has more than 11 years of experience in academics. He has published and presented several research papers in various international journals and conferences of repute. He is an active member of the Universal Association of Computer and Electronics Engineers.

name: Pankaj Pant
school: School of Pharmaceutical & Population Health Informatics
qualification: M.Pharm, Ph.D
research_interests: Reproductive Health, Metabolic Disorders, Cancer, Neuropsychological Health
bio: Dr. Pankaj Pant holds a B. Pharm and an M. Pharm in Pharmacology from Uttarakhand Technical University and completed his Ph.D. at DIT University, Dehradun, supported by grants from Bayer Pharmaceutical, Germany. His Ph.D. research, titled "Key Markers Differentially Expressed in Cumulus and Mural Cells of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) Patients," was conducted under the guidance of Prof. (Dr.) Havagiray Chitme, a renowned researcher and academician in the field of pharmacy.
